title: MD
biography: Charles Drew was an American surgeon and researcher who developed improved techniques for blood storage and organized the first large-scale blood banking programs during World War II. He directed the 'Blood for Britain' project and the American Red Cross blood bank, and was the first African American to earn a Doctor of Science degree.
aiSummary: Charles Drew was a surgery specialist who pioneered blood banking and blood plasma storage techniques during WWII. First African American to earn a Doctor of Science degree. Based in Washington, United States.
techniquesInvented: Large-scale blood plasma processing and storage

citations.title: Banked blood: a study in blood preservation

awards: E.S. Jones Award for Research in Medical Science

timeline.title: Directed Blood for Britain project
timeline.description: Organized the first mass blood collection and shipment program to supply British hospitals during WWII

timeline.title: Directed American Red Cross blood bank
timeline.description: Became first director of the American Red Cross blood bank program

timeline.title: Resigned over racial segregation of blood
timeline.description: Protested the Red Cross policy of segregating blood by race, calling it scientifically baseless

timeline.title: Head of Surgery at Howard University
timeline.description: Became professor and head of the Department of Surgery at Howard University
